 {"id":"bd-2k018.18","title":"Table layout correctness unit tests (widget + markdown)","description":"Purpose:
- Add non-snapshot unit tests for table layout correctness across widget + markdown paths.

Scope:
- Widget tables: verify column width calculation with padding + column_gap + row_height; clipping behavior for narrow widths; wide-glyph width correctness; alignment in cells.
- Markdown tables: verify alignment rules (left/center/right), escaped pipe handling, multi-line cells, width truncation with ellipsis, and border alignment.
- Use deterministic fixtures; assert computed widths/positions rather than visual snapshots.

Logging:
- On failure, log table dims, computed widths, and offending cell content.

Acceptance:
- Layout invariants are validated independent of snapshots.
- Tests fail with actionable logs when layout math regresses.

 {"id":"bd-2k018.19","title":"Cross-render parity tests (widget vs markdown tables)","description":"Purpose:
- Verify that widget tables and markdown tables produce equivalent styling and layout under the same TableTheme.

Scope:
- Render identical table data through both paths and compare:
  - column widths
  - row heights
  - resolved style hashes per row/section
- Use fixed phase to ensure deterministic comparison.

Logging:
- On failure, log per-row style hashes, column widths, and offending section.

Acceptance:
- Parity tests prove markdown + widget tables are thematically consistent.

 {"id":"bd-2k018.2","title":"Design TableTheme API + presets (spec)","description":"Purpose:
- Specify the TableTheme API and presets so implementation is straightforward and consistent.

API sketch (to be finalized in implementation):
- struct TableTheme { border, header, row, row_alt, row_selected, row_hover, divider, padding, column_gap, row_height, effects: Vec<TableEffectRule>, preset_id }
- enum TableSection { Header, Body, Footer }
- enum TableEffectTarget { Section(TableSection), Row(usize), RowRange{start,end}, Column(usize), ColumnRange{start,end}, AllRows, AllCells }
- enum TableEffect { Pulse{fg_a, fg_b, bg_a, bg_b, speed, phase_offset}, BreathingGlow{fg, bg, intensity, speed, phase_offset, asymmetry}, GradientSweep{gradient, speed, phase_offset} }
- struct TableEffectRule { target, effect, priority, blend_mode, style_mask }
- TableTheme::resolve_style(ctx, phase) -> Style (deterministic, low-cost)

Index semantics (must be explicit in spec):
- Row/column indices are 0-based and refer to body rows/columns (header row is not counted as row 0).
- Header effects should target Section(Header) explicitly.
- AllRows targets body rows only (excludes header); AllCells includes header + body.

Phase semantics:
- Phase input is normalized to [0,1). Resolver should define clamp/wrap semantics (e.g., fract for >1) and document it.

Priority semantics:
- Effects apply in ascending priority, then stable insertion order to keep determinism when priorities tie.

Ergonomics:
- Builder helpers: with_border/with_header/with_effect/preset(...)
- Re-export from ftui prelude or theme module for easy access.
- Accept either ColorToken-based Styles or PackedRgba styles (so themes can be dynamic).

Presets (expanded):
- aurora: luminous header bar + cool zebra + crisp borders.
- graphite: ultra-legible monochrome for data density.
- neon: accent header + subtle color sweep for emphasis.
- slate: subdued neutral for serious dashboards.
- solar: warm, high‑contrast data tables (daylight friendly).
- orchard: muted green accents for financial/ops tables.
- paper: light, minimal, print‑like appearance.
- midnight: dark, restrained palette with low‑glare contrasts.
- terminal_classic: ANSI‑heritage palette; uses 16‑color baseline, but may upgrade to 256/truecolor when available to avoid degraded look on modern terminals.

Constraints:
- Deterministic: resolve_style is pure; phase is passed in explicitly (no internal clocks or RNG).
- Degradation-aware: TableTheme must respect Frame.degradation.apply_styling (no styling when degraded).
- Performance: no allocations or string ops in render hot paths; resolve is O(rules).

Merge / precedence rules (must be explicit in spec):
- Base style from theme (header/row/row_alt) then row_selected / row_hover overlays.
- Explicit Row::style and per-cell Span style must remain honored; define merge order so explicit cell styles are not accidentally overwritten.
- Effects apply last using style_mask to limit which channels (fg/bg/attrs) they may override.

Border mapping (widget tables):
- TableTheme.border + divider should map to Block border styles where Table uses Block.
- Block title styling remains independent; theme should not clobber titles.

Glyph policy:
- TableTheme controls style only; border glyph characters are unchanged unless explicitly extended later.

Acceptance:
- Spec is self-contained and includes fields, effect model, precedence, determinism + perf constraints, index + phase semantics, and border mapping semantics.
","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-04T18:40:32.372779939Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T19:15:38.656516770Z","closed_at":"2026-02-04T19:15:05.484899699Z","close_reason":"Spec drafted in docs/specs/table-theme.md","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2k018.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2k018","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T18:40:32.372779939Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2k018.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2k018.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T18:41:14.075933856Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":630,"issue_id":"bd-2k018.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"TableTheme API spec (draft)

Goals
- Single style source for ftui-widgets Table + markdown tables
- Deterministic and phase-driven; no internal time or randomness
- Low-cost resolve path; no heap allocations on hot render path

Core types
1) struct TableTheme
- preset_id: &'static str (or TablePreset enum)
- border: Style (outer border)
- divider: Style (inner borders between columns/rows)
- header: Style
- row: Style
- row_alt: Style
- row_selected: Style
- row_hover: Style
- padding_x: u16 (cells left/right padding)
- padding_y: u16 (extra row height, or use row_height)
- column_gap: u16 (maps to Table column_spacing)
- row_height: u16 (default 1)
- effects: Vec<TableEffectRule>

2) enum TableSection { Header, Body, Footer }

3) struct TableContext
- section: TableSection
- row: usize
- col: usize
- is_header: bool
- is_selected: bool
- is_hovered: bool
- is_alt_row: bool
- is_divider: bool (optional, for border/divider styling)

4) enum TableEffectTarget
- Section(TableSection)
- Row(usize)
- RowRange { start, end }
- Column(usize)
- ColumnRange { start, end }
- AllRows
- AllCells

5) enum TableEffect
- Pulse { fg_a, fg_b, bg_a, bg_b, speed, phase }
- BreathingGlow { fg, bg, intensity, speed, phase, asymmetry }
- GradientSweep { gradient, speed, phase }

6) struct TableEffectRule
- target: TableEffectTarget
- effect: TableEffect
- style_mask: StyleMask (optional; fg/bg/attrs selection)

Resolve API
- fn resolve_style(&self, ctx: TableContext, phase: f32) -> Style
- Composition order (deterministic):
  - base = if ctx.is_header { header } else if ctx.is_alt_row { row_alt } else { row }
  - base = if ctx.is_selected { row_selected.merge(&base) } else base
  - base = if ctx.is_hovered { row_hover.merge(&base) } else base
  - for rule in effects matching ctx: base = effect_style(rule, phase).merge(&base)
  - return base
- Optional fn resolve_border_style(kind: BorderKind) -> Style
  - BorderKind: OuterTop/OuterMid/OuterBottom/InnerVertical/InnerHorizontal

Determinism constraints
- resolve_style must be pure: output depends only on ctx + phase + theme
- phase is provided by caller (e.g., normalized 0..1 or ticks); no internal clocks
- effects must not mutate state; no RNG
- markdown table rendering should pass a stable phase or 0.0

Performance constraints
- resolve_style should not allocate; prefer precomputed color steps for effects
- effect evaluation should be O(rules) with simple condition checks
- merging uses Style::merge; avoid string allocations or span cloning inside resolve

Presets (initial)
- aurora: bright header bar, cool zebra, crisp borders
- graphite: monochrome, high-contrast, dense data
- neon: accent header, subtle sweep on selected row only

Integration notes
- Table widget: use TableTheme for base row/header/selected/hover; padding_x replaces hardcoded cell spacing; column_gap maps to Table::column_spacing
- Markdown tables: replace MarkdownTheme table_* with TableTheme (border/divider/header/row/row_alt); table_max_width still applies
- Effects are opt-in: markdown uses phase=0.0; Table widget can accept phase from runtime or omit

-{"id":"bd-2k018.20","title":"TableTheme diagnostics + style-hash utilities","description":"Purpose:
- Centralize deterministic style hashing + diagnostics for table theming so unit tests, snapshots, and E2E logs are consistent and actionable.

Scope:
- Add helper to compute a stable hash for resolved TableTheme styles (row/section/border).
- Add debug formatter that prints key style fields (fg/bg/attrs) for failure logs.
- Ensure hashing does not allocate in hot paths; used only in tests/E2E.

Usage:
- Unit tests: log style hash + formatted fields on failure.
- Snapshot tests: include style hash in snapshot metadata.
- E2E: JSONL logs include style hash + formatted summary.

Acceptance:
- All table theming tests and E2E logs share a single, deterministic style-hash implementation.

 {"id":"bd-2k018.21","title":"Theme override cookbook + preset selection guidance","description":"Purpose:
- Provide practical guidance for users to customize TableTheme and choose presets in real-world scenarios.

Scope:
- Add a small cookbook section (docs/examples) covering:

 {"id":"bd-2k018.22","title":"Preset preview gallery screen (demo showcase)","description":"Purpose:
- Provide a dedicated demo screen that previews all TableTheme presets side‑by‑side so users can quickly evaluate aesthetics and legibility.

Background / rationale:
- Users requested more presets and explicit guidance; a gallery makes selection obvious and accelerates adoption.
- A gallery also acts as a regression surface for theme changes across terminal sizes.

Scope:
- Add a showcase screen that renders the same dataset across all presets.
- Include both widget Table and markdown Table representations if feasible, to confirm visual parity.
- Display preset name + a short descriptor (e.g., “solar: warm/high‑contrast”).
- For terminal_classic, show ANSI baseline with modern‑terminal enhancement (if available) to ensure it doesn’t look worse on modern terminals.

Test surfaces:
- Add snapshots for the gallery at 80x24 and 120x40.
- Ensure gallery is included in E2E logs (preset_id, phase, style hash, column widths).

Constraints:
- Deterministic phase for any animation in gallery.
- Avoid heavy per‑cell computations; reuse existing render paths.

Acceptance:
- Gallery screen exists and is discoverable from demo navigation.
- All presets render legibly at both sizes.
- Widget/markdown parity is visually consistent.
- Snapshot + E2E logs capture the gallery.

 {"id":"bd-2k018.23","title":"Interactive preset gallery controls (keyboard + live overrides)","description":"Purpose:
- Make the preset preview gallery highly interactive so users can explore presets and live overrides without leaving the screen.

Scope:
- Keyboard controls to cycle presets and toggle between widget/markdown view.
- Live override toggles (e.g., header emphasis, zebra strength, border style, highlight row).
- On‑screen help legend for controls.
- Persist selection per session so exploration feels continuous.

Testing:
- Snapshot tests for default state (controls visible, deterministic).
- E2E logs include selected preset, toggles state, and current view mode.

Acceptance:
- Gallery is fully navigable by keyboard.
- Live overrides update immediately and deterministically.
- E2E logs capture interactive state for debugging.

@@ -413,7 +413,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-2lwf","title":"Time-Travel Debugging with Frame Snapshots","description":"# Time-Travel Debugging with Frame Snapshots

## Background & Motivation

Visual bugs are notoriously hard to debug. By the time you notice something wrong, the state that caused it is gone. Traditional logging captures events but not visual state.

**Why this matters:**
- Layout bugs manifest visually, not in logs
- Race conditions cause intermittent visual glitches
- Users report "I saw X but now it shows Y" - impossible to repro
- Debugging requires exact visual state at bug time

**The solution:**
- Record frame snapshots (compressed) during development
- Enable "rewind" to inspect past visual states
- Export session recordings for bug reports
- Integrate with ProgramSimulator for deterministic replay

**Relationship to existing beads:**
- Extends bd-10i.8.3 (ProgramSimulator) with visual snapshots
- Enhances bd-2u4 (snapshot/golden testing) with runtime recording

## Technical Design

### Core Types

```rust
/// Time-travel recording session
pub struct TimeTravel {
    /// Circular buffer of frame snapshots
    snapshots: VecDeque<CompressedFrame>,
    /// Maximum snapshots to keep
    capacity: usize,
    /// Frame counter
    frame_number: u64,
    /// Whether recording is active
    recording: bool,
    /// Metadata for each frame
    metadata: VecDeque<FrameMetadata>,
}

/// Compressed frame (delta-encoded from previous)
pub struct CompressedFrame {
    /// Base frame number (for delta)
    base: Option<u64>,
    /// Changed cells (sparse)
    changes: Vec<CellChange>,
    /// Checksum for integrity
    checksum: u32,
}

pub struct CellChange {
    x: u16,
    y: u16,
    cell: Cell,
}

/// Metadata attached to each frame
pub struct FrameMetadata {
    /// Frame number
    frame: u64,
    /// Timestamp
    timestamp: Instant,
    /// Events that triggered this frame
    events: Vec<Event>,
    /// Model hash (for identifying state)
    model_hash: u64,
    /// Render duration
    render_time: Duration,
}
```

### Recording API

```rust
impl TimeTravel {
    /// Create a new recorder with given capacity
    pub fn new(capacity: usize) -> Self {
        Self {
            snapshots: VecDeque::with_capacity(capacity),
            capacity,
            frame_number: 0,
            recording: true,
            metadata: VecDeque::with_capacity(capacity),
        }
    }
    
    /// Record a frame (call from runtime after each present)
    pub fn record(&mut self, frame: &Frame, events: &[Event], render_time: Duration) {
        if \!self.recording {
            return;
        }
        
        let compressed = if let Some(prev) = self.snapshots.back() {
            // Delta-encode from previous
            CompressedFrame::delta(frame, prev)
        } else {
            // First frame: full snapshot
            CompressedFrame::full(frame)
        };
        
        // Evict oldest if at capacity
        if self.snapshots.len() >= self.capacity {
            self.snapshots.pop_front();
            self.metadata.pop_front();
        }
        
        let metadata = FrameMetadata {
            frame: self.frame_number,
            timestamp: Instant::now(),
            events: events.to_vec(),
            model_hash: frame.model_hash(),
            render_time,
        };
        
        self.snapshots.push_back(compressed);
        self.metadata.push_back(metadata);
        self.frame_number += 1;
    }
    
    /// Get current recording size
    pub fn len(&self) -> usize {
        self.snapshots.len()
    }
    
    /// Pause/resume recording
    pub fn set_recording(&mut self, recording: bool) {
        self.recording = recording;
    }
}
```

### Replay API

```rust
impl TimeTravel {
    /// Get frame at specific index (0 = oldest recorded)
    pub fn get(&self, index: usize) -> Option<Frame> {
        if index >= self.snapshots.len() {
            return None;
        }
        
        // Reconstruct frame by applying deltas
        let mut frame = Frame::default();
        for i in 0..=index {
            self.snapshots[i].apply_to(&mut frame);
        }
        Some(frame)
    }
    
    /// Get frame N steps back from current
    pub fn rewind(&self, steps: usize) -> Option<Frame> {
        let index = self.snapshots.len().checked_sub(steps + 1)?;
        self.get(index)
    }
    
    /// Get metadata for frame
    pub fn metadata(&self, index: usize) -> Option<&FrameMetadata> {
        self.metadata.get(index)
    }
    
    /// Find frame by model hash
    pub fn find_by_hash(&self, hash: u64) -> Option<usize> {
        self.metadata.iter().position(|m| m.model_hash == hash)
    }
    
    /// Get all frames between two points
    pub fn range(&self, start: usize, end: usize) -> Vec<Frame> {
        (start..end.min(self.snapshots.len()))
            .filter_map(|i| self.get(i))
            .collect()
    }
}
```

### Compression Strategy

```rust
impl CompressedFrame {
    /// Create delta-encoded frame
    pub fn delta(current: &Frame, previous: &CompressedFrame) -> Self {
        let prev_frame = previous.decompress();
        let mut changes = Vec::new();
        
        for y in 0..current.height() {
            for x in 0..current.width() {
                let curr_cell = current.get(x, y);
                let prev_cell = prev_frame.get(x, y);
                
                if \!curr_cell.bits_eq(prev_cell) {
                    changes.push(CellChange { x, y, cell: *curr_cell });
                }
            }
        }
        
        Self {
            base: Some(previous.frame_number()),
            changes,
            checksum: current.checksum(),
        }
    }
    
    /// Apply changes to reconstruct frame
    pub fn apply_to(&self, frame: &mut Frame) {
        for change in &self.changes {
            frame.set(change.x, change.y, change.cell);
        }
    }
    
    /// Estimated memory usage
    pub fn memory_size(&self) -> usize {
        std::mem::size_of::<Self>() + 
        self.changes.len() * std::mem::size_of::<CellChange>()
    }
}
```

### Export/Import

```rust
impl TimeTravel {
    /// Export recording to file (for bug reports)
    pub fn export(&self, path: &Path) -> io::Result<()> {
        let file = File::create(path)?;
        let mut writer = GzEncoder::new(file, Compression::default());
        
        // Write header
        writer.write_all(b"FTUI-TTRAVEL-V1\\n")?;
        
        // Write frames
        for (snapshot, metadata) in self.snapshots.iter().zip(self.metadata.iter()) {
            bincode::serialize_into(&mut writer, &(snapshot, metadata))?;
        }
        
        Ok(())
    }
    
    /// Import recording from file
    pub fn import(path: &Path) -> io::Result<Self> {
        let file = File::open(path)?;
        let mut reader = GzDecoder::new(file);
        
        // Read and validate header
        let mut header = [0u8; 16];
        reader.read_exact(&mut header)?;
        if \!header.starts_with(b"FTUI-TTRAVEL-V1") {
            return Err(io::Error::new(io::ErrorKind::InvalidData, "Invalid header"));
        }
        
        // Read frames
        let mut time_travel = Self::new(1000);
        while let Ok((snapshot, metadata)) = bincode::deserialize_from(&mut reader) {
            time_travel.snapshots.push_back(snapshot);
            time_travel.metadata.push_back(metadata);
        }
        
        Ok(time_travel)
    }
}
```

### Debug UI Integration

```rust
/// Overlay widget for time-travel inspection
pub struct TimeTravelInspector {
    time_travel: Arc<RwLock<TimeTravel>>,
    current_index: usize,
    playing: bool,
    playback_speed: f32,
}

impl TimeTravelInspector {
    pub fn render(&self, area: Rect, buf: &mut Buffer) {
        let tt = self.time_travel.read().unwrap();
        
        // Render timeline bar
        let timeline_area = Rect::new(area.x, area.bottom() - 1, area.width, 1);
        self.render_timeline(&tt, timeline_area, buf);
        
        // Render frame preview
        if let Some(frame) = tt.get(self.current_index) {
            let preview_area = Rect::new(area.x, area.y, area.width, area.height - 2);
            frame.render(preview_area, buf);
        }
        
        // Render metadata
        if let Some(meta) = tt.metadata(self.current_index) {
            let info = format\!(
                "Frame {} | Events: {} | Render: {:?}",
                meta.frame,
                meta.events.len(),
                meta.render_time
            );
            buf.set_string(area.x, area.bottom() - 2, &info, Style::default());
        }
    }
    
    pub fn handle_event(&mut self, event: Event) {
        match event {
            Event::Key(KeyCode::Left, _) => self.step_back(),
            Event::Key(KeyCode::Right, _) => self.step_forward(),
            Event::Key(KeyCode::Space, _) => self.toggle_playback(),
            Event::Key(KeyCode::Home, _) => self.jump_to_start(),
            Event::Key(KeyCode::End, _) => self.jump_to_end(),
            _ => {}
        }
    }
}
```

## Test Plan

### Unit Tests
- [ ] record() stores frame correctly
- [ ] Circular buffer evicts oldest at capacity
- [ ] Delta compression produces smaller data
- [ ] get() reconstructs frame correctly
- [ ] rewind() returns correct frame
- [ ] Export/import roundtrips correctly

### Integration Tests
- [ ] Recording during live session works
- [ ] Inspector UI renders frames correctly
- [ ] Playback at different speeds works
- [ ] Memory stays bounded during long sessions

### Property Tests
- [ ] get(i) always equals original frame at i
- [ ] Delta + apply is identity
- [ ] Capacity is never exceeded

## Memory Budgets
- Default capacity: 1000 frames
- Typical delta size: ~100 bytes (5% cells changed)
- Total memory: ~100KB for 1000 frames

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] TimeTravel type implemented
- [ ] CompressedFrame with delta encoding
- [ ] Recording during runtime
- [ ] Rewind API works
- [ ] Export/import for bug reports
- [ ] TimeTravelInspector debug UI
- [ ] Memory budget enforced
- [ ] All tests pass","notes":"## Thread Safety Note

The TimeTravelInspector uses Arc<RwLock<TimeTravel>>. Current implementation uses unwrap():

```rust
let tt = self.time_travel.read().unwrap();
```

**Issue:** This can panic if the lock is poisoned (another thread panicked while holding it).

**Fix:** Use expect() with context or handle the error:

```rust
// Option 1: expect with context
let tt = self.time_travel.read()
    .expect("TimeTravel lock poisoned");

// Option 2: Handle gracefully
let tt = match self.time_travel.read() {
    Ok(guard) => guard,
    Err(poisoned) => {
        tracing::warn!("TimeTravel lock was poisoned, recovering");
        poisoned.into_inner()
    }
};
```

The second approach allows recovery from poisoned locks, which is more robust for debugging tools.

## Logging Requirements

```rust
tracing::info!(recording = enabled, "Time-travel recording toggled");
tracing::trace!(frame_number = frame, compressed_size = size, "Frame recorded");
tracing::debug!(capacity = capacity, "Ring buffer full, evicting oldest");
tracing::debug!(from = current, to = target, "Time-travel seek");
tracing::info!(path = ?path, frames = count, "Export/import complete");

 {"id":"bd-2m5","title":"SIMD-accelerated cell comparison and buffer operations","description":"# SIMD/autovec accelerated hot paths (NO unsafe)

## Objective
Improve hot-path performance (cell comparisons, clears, copies, ASCII width checks) **without introducing unsafe code**.

Project constraint: `#![forbid(unsafe_code)]` across our crates. We must not create an “unsafe-only SIMD crate”.

## Plan Alignment
The plan mentions optional SIMD acceleration. In this repo, that must translate into one of:
- compiler autovectorization via carefully-written scalar loops
- safe portable SIMD (only if available without `unsafe` in our Rust toolchain)
- leveraging well-vetted dependencies that encapsulate unsafe internally (our crate remains `forbid(unsafe_code)`)

## Scope
Target the following operations, in priority order:
1. Cell equality comparisons in diff scan
2. Buffer clear/fill
3. Row/rect copies
4. ASCII-width fast paths

## Strategy
- Write scalar loops that are friendly to autovec:
  - contiguous slices
  - minimal branching
  - row-major iteration
- Use benchmark-driven iteration:
  - measure p50/p95 for 80x24 and larger terminals
  - confirm no regressions in correctness tests

## Explicit Non-Goal
- Adding `unsafe` blocks or `#[target_feature]` functions inside ftui code.

If later we decide explicit unsafe SIMD is required, that needs an explicit policy change (and likely an ADR + safety review).

## Tests Required
- Property tests confirming optimized paths exactly match the reference scalar behavior.
- Benchmarks that show improvements and include regression budgets.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Optimizations preserve correctness (property tests pass; byte-for-byte identical results where applicable).
- [ ] Benchmarks demonstrate measurable improvements on at least one key hot path.
- [ ] No unsafe code is introduced in ftui crates.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-31T22:20:50.805814276Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T19:51:43.104701371Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T19:51:43.104678769Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2m5","depends_on_id":"bd-10i.3.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:17Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2mc1","title":"Semantic Event System: High-Level Input Abstraction","description":"## Background

FrankenTUI has excellent low-level input parsing in `ftui-core/src/input_parser.rs` that transforms raw terminal bytes into `Event` types (Key, Mouse, Resize, etc.). However, **every widget must implement its own gesture detection** for common patterns like:

- Double-click detection (file browser, list selection)
- Long-press recognition (context menus)
- Drag operations (text selection, list reordering)
- Chord sequences (Ctrl+K Ctrl+C style shortcuts)
- Swipe gestures (touch terminal support)

This leads to **duplicated, inconsistent, and often missing** gesture handling across widgets.

## Problem Statement

Consider implementing double-click in a List widget:
1. Track last click time and position
2. Define timeout threshold (300ms typical)
3. Handle edge cases (different positions, mouse up/down)
4. This code is repeated in Table, FilePicker, TreeView...

The same applies to drag detection, long-press, and chords.

## Solution: Semantic Event Layer

Add a `GestureRecognizer` in `ftui-runtime` that:
1. Receives raw `Event` from terminal
2. Maintains state (click history, mouse position, key sequences)
3. Emits `SemanticEvent` when gestures are recognized
4. Widgets receive high-level events instead of raw input

## Proposed API

```rust
// New event types in ftui-core
pub enum SemanticEvent {
    // Mouse gestures
    Click { pos: Position, button: MouseButton },
    DoubleClick { pos: Position, button: MouseButton },
    TripleClick { pos: Position, button: MouseButton }, // Line selection
    LongPress { pos: Position, duration: Duration },
    
    // Drag gestures
    DragStart { pos: Position, button: MouseButton },
    DragMove { from: Position, to: Position, delta: (i16, i16) },
    DragEnd { from: Position, to: Position },
    DragCancel,
    
    // Keyboard gestures  
    Chord { sequence: Vec<KeyEvent> }, // e.g., Ctrl+K Ctrl+C
    
    // Touch-like gestures (for touch terminals)
    Swipe { direction: Direction, distance: u16, velocity: f32 },
    Pinch { scale: f32 }, // Future: zoom
}

// Recognizer configuration
pub struct GestureConfig {
    pub double_click_timeout: Duration,  // Default: 300ms
    pub long_press_threshold: Duration,  // Default: 500ms
    pub drag_threshold: u16,             // Default: 3 cells
    pub chord_timeout: Duration,         // Default: 1000ms
    pub enable_swipe: bool,              // Default: false
}

// Stateful recognizer
pub struct GestureRecognizer {
    config: GestureConfig,
    // Internal state
    last_click: Option<(Instant, Position, MouseButton)>,
    drag_state: Option<DragState>,
    chord_buffer: Vec<KeyEvent>,
    chord_start: Option<Instant>,
}

impl GestureRecognizer {
    pub fn process(&mut self, event: Event) -> Vec<SemanticEvent>;
    pub fn reset(&mut self);
    pub fn is_dragging(&self) -> bool;
}
```

## Integration with Runtime

```rust
// In Program::run()
loop {
    let raw_event = terminal.read_event()?;
    
    // Process through gesture recognizer
    let semantic_events = self.gesture_recognizer.process(raw_event.clone());
    
    // Model receives both raw and semantic events
    for semantic in semantic_events {
        let msg = Message::Semantic(semantic);
        self.model.update(msg);
    }
    
    // Raw event still available if needed
    let msg = Message::from(raw_event);
    self.model.update(msg);
}
```

## Benefits

1. **Consistency**: All widgets get the same gesture detection
2. **Correctness**: Edge cases handled once, correctly
3. **Configurability**: Users can tune thresholds
4. **Extensibility**: New gestures added in one place
5. **Testing**: Gesture recognition is unit-testable

## Performance Considerations

- GestureRecognizer maintains minimal state (~100 bytes)
- Processing is O(1) per event
- No allocations on hot path (events returned via SmallVec)
- Configurable to disable unused gesture types

## Files to Create/Modify

- crates/ftui-core/src/semantic_event.rs (NEW)
- crates/ftui-core/src/gesture.rs (NEW)
- crates/ftui-runtime/src/program.rs (integrate)
- crates/ftui-core/src/lib.rs (exports)

## Success Metrics

- Zero duplicated gesture detection in widgets
- All existing widgets migrated to semantic events
- Gesture recognition tests cover edge cases
- No performance regression in event processing

## Success Criteria
- Epic delivers a cohesive user-visible capability: Semantic Event System: High-Level Input Abstraction
- Child tasks are not just implemented; each has unit tests + E2E scripts with verbose JSONL logs and deterministic fixtures
- Graceful degradation tiers are specified (capabilities, fallbacks, safety) and validated in tests
- Formal invariants documented + enforced via property tests; any dynamic decisions have an evidence ledger and debug UI


## Alien Artifact Upgrades
- Model the core decision(s) with explicit state, priors/assumptions, and invariants; write down failure modes as first-class artifacts.
- Explainability: add an evidence ledger for any non-trivial decision (why this branch, why this fallback). Prefer Bayes factors / log-likelihood contributions over ad-hoc scoring.
- Guarantees: where prediction/thresholding exists, prefer distribution-free calibration (conformal prediction) or anytime-valid testing (e-process) so we can stop early without invalid inference.
- Graceful degradation: define capability tiers + deterministic fallbacks; never fail closed without a traceable reason.


## Extreme Optimization
- Baseline first: capture p50/p95/p99 for relevant command(s) with `hyperfine` (store raw output).
- Profile next: CPU flamegraph + allocation profile; identify top-5 hotspots.
- Opportunity matrix: implement only Score ≥ 2.0 (Impact×Confidence/Effort).
- Isomorphism proof per change: ordering, tie-breaking, RNG seeds, and golden checksums must match.
- Regression gates: add microbench + budgets; failures must print actionable deltas and include log artifacts.


## Testing Plan
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures.
- Property tests: invariants (idempotence, monotonicity, determinism) where applicable.
- Fuzz tests: parsers/state machines and any input-facing logic.
- Determinism: fixed seeds/time-freeze; snapshot fixtures must be stable.
- Metamorphic tests: shrink/grow, reorder non-semantic inputs, and ensure invariants still hold.

 {"id":"bd-2mlp","title":"Implement Frame Caching tests","description":"# Implement Frame Caching tests

## Background
Part of Frame Caching with Smart Invalidation (bd-6qxh). Tests ensure caching works correctly.

## Unit Tests
- cached_widget_reuses_buffer: Second render uses cached buffer
- mark_dirty_triggers_rerender: Dirty flag forces fresh render
- resize_invalidates_cache: Area change triggers rerender
- hash_detects_content_change: Content change detected and rerendered

## Property Tests
- cache_never_returns_stale_content: After mark_dirty, output is fresh
- resize_always_triggers_rerender: Any area change = fresh render

## Integration Tests
- static_header_cached_effectively: Header only rendered once per resize
